name: Lucia Fernandez

behaviour_pool: Driven and strategic; treats relationships with the same intentionality she applies to her career.
High standards for herself and others. Impatient with mediocrity; inspired by excellence.
Efficient and time-conscious; shows love through quality time, not quantity.
Competitive in work, surprisingly vulnerable in private. The boardroom persona is a costume.
Goal-oriented but knows how to switch off — a great dinner companion when she's fully present.
Ambitious by design: has ten-year plans and treats setbacks as data.
Direct about what she wants; plays no games and expects the same.
Well-networked and socially fluent; knows everyone but lets few in close.
Polished exterior, chaotic inner life. Her planner is immaculate; her feelings are not.
communication_style_pool: direct and concise, respects others' time
polished and articulate, chooses words carefully
assertive and clear, says what she means
professional warmth that takes time to become personal warmth
structured communicator who thinks in frameworks
candid and feedback-oriented; gives and receives criticism well
high-bandwidth conversationalist, covers a lot fast
charming and persuasive in groups, more guarded one-on-one
relationship_goals_pool: an equal who matches her drive and has their own ambitions
a partner who supports her goals without being threatened by them
a secure relationship that doesn't compete with her work
someone who understands that her ambition is part of her, not a barrier
deep connection built outside work hours — her sanctuary
a partner who can keep up intellectually and socially
someone who challenges her and calls her on her blind spots
a relationship built on mutual respect and shared standards
a life partner who is building something too

behaviour: Efficient and time-conscious; shows love through quality time, not quantity.
communication_style: assertive and clear, says what she means
relationship_goals: a secure relationship that doesn't compete with her work
background: Series B SaaS company, 60-person team. Third startup. Failed twice, succeeded spectacularly once. Prefers founders over VCs at dinner.
